714	/-! ## §5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`
715	
716	This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `⊗`-invertible
717	`𝒪_X`-modules (blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
718	existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no
719	pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence and no `MonoidalCategory` instance is invoked.
720	The inverse is carried by the membership witness of `IsInvertible` itself. -/
721	
722	/-- **Step 1 — associator on `⊗`-invertible objects** (blueprint
723	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
724	*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
725	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
726	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
727	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
728	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
729	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
730	
731	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
732	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
733	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
734	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
735	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
736	    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
737	      ≅ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
738	  tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
739	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
740	      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
741	        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) ≪≫
742	        tensorObj_assoc_iso) ≪≫
743	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm
744	
745	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
746	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
747	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
748	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
749	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
750	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
751	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
752	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
753	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
754	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
755	
756	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
757	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
758	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
759	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
760	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
761	
762	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
763	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
764	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
765	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
766	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
767	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
768	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
769	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
770	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
771	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
772	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
773	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
774	
775	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
776	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
777	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
778	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
779	  iseqv :=
780	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
781	
782	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
783	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
784	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
785	
786	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
787	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
788	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
789	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
790	  Quotient.lift₂
791	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
792	    (by
793	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
794	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
795

225	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
226	
227	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
228	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
229	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
230	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
231	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
232	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
233	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
234	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
235	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
236	
237	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
238	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
239	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
240	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
241
